title: Representative Eval Sets, Deterministic Oracles, Baselines, and Versions · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers representative eval sets, deterministic oracles, baselines, and versions into a changed versioned representative agent evaluation corpus. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned representative agent evaluation corpus with automated and judgment evidence. Learners pr…
objectives: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.
Define exact schema, state, tool, argument, authorization, effect, citation, budget, stop, and recovery assertions wherever outcomes are deterministic.
Create changed-input, changed-policy, tool-fault, injection, memory-poisoning, approval, cancellation, and resource-exhaustion cases without leaking answers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Eval Task Distribution
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 9443 during a controlled support-operations rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines; model output and tool arguments remain untrusted until validated; then introduce a sensitive action, poisoned memory, leaking log, or computer-use request and deny or pause it. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Sample normal, boundary, ambiguous, multilingual, accessibility, refusal, tool, failure, adversarial, and recovery cases from the intended task distribution.

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-eval-version-provenance-1mwzlb" that uses case 4888 to build a versioned representative evaluation set with deterministic oracles, changed cases, adversarial cases, accessibility needs, and baselines. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-eval-version-provenance-1mwzlb
# Competency: agentpy-eval-version-provenance.
# Case 4888: an accessible public-service intake assistant.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: change one eval case, rubric, model version, token price, rate limit, or latency distribution and rerun release gates.
def evidence_agentpy_eval_version_provenance_1mwzlb(eval_cases: int = 72, normal_cases: int = 30, boundary_cases: int = 18, adversarial_cases: int = 18, accessibility_cases: int = 6) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mwzlb = "1mwzlb-blzwm1-4888"
    assert evidence_variant_1mwzlb
    evidence_axes_1mwzlb = (
        "authority1mwzlb",
        "interaction4888",
        "toolblzwm1",
        "state1mwzlb4888",
        "evaluation48881mwzlb",
        "recoveryblzwm14888",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mwzlb)) == 6
    if eval_cases < 40:
        return False, eval_cases, "eval-depth"
    if normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases != eval_cases:
        return False, normal_cases, "eval-reconciliation"
    if boundary_cases < 8 or adversarial_cases < 8:
        return False, adversarial_cases, "eval-risk-coverage"
    if accessibility_cases < 4:
        return False, accessibility_cases, "eval-accessibility"
    assert normal_cases + boundary_cases + adversarial_cases + accessibility_cases == eval_cases
    return True, eval_cases, "representative-agent-evals"
